Let's cookin' good lookin'. Okay, Facebook group management. How do you manage the Facebook group so that you can not want to kill yourself? All right, so here's the deal. What you want to do is create a Facebook group if you do not already have one. If you already have one with your existing customers, I would recommend putting your challengers in that group. Some people keep it separate, but in my experience, most people's customer groups are not super engaged. Most people, not all of them, but most people don't have super engaged customer groups. So as soon as you have the challengers in one of your customer groups, then the engagement from your current customers actually goes up. So they get more excited because these clients are posting pictures of their food, they're posting recipe ideas, they're talking about progress, and the vibe of the group blows up and it's great. So that's my opinion. Now within the group, you're gonna have a pin post, which is the second point here. These are the rules that I would always have within that post. First, no complaining. Second, no business talk if someone talks about pricing or memberships or expensive, blah, blah, blah, anything that is business related, they can talk to us personally. And then third rule is just stay positive, don't be a dick. There's a difference between asking for help and complaining. And we prefer the, or not prefer, we say the latter is cool, the former is not. Now, billing contacts, you just wanna make sure that they know who they can reach out to there so that they can save it and you should put save, save this contact info in your phone so you have it. The class times, because everyone always asks what the class times are, especially on weekends, what's the class time on Sundays? Right, so just post it on there and tell people to screenshot it. How to reach support, if you have support or you have your accountability coach, which is the last point I have there. How to contact that person, just whatever rules, if they lost the contact, how to re-get it. And then link to Facebook business page for check-in. So have the link to the business page so that they know which page they should be checking into to count their workouts towards their detox, their challenge, their whatever. All right, so hope that makes sense. And that is how you manage the Facebook stuff the easiest. As you may have seen with an art group, we prefer to have managed groups, meaning we approve posts. It's just much easier to manage the vibe with a group that way. That's just been our experience. And then if someone has something that shouldn't be a public thing, we can catch it, we can just message them personally. And that way it just kind of avoids unnecessary drama. So that is what my recommendation is and it is up to you.
